title: Scale-Invariant Open Quantum Systems
authors: Carlos Argüelles, Gabriela Barenboim, Gonzalo Herrera, Tanvi Krishnan, Héctor Sanchis
abstract: We develop a complete theoretical framework for open quantum systems coupled to scale-invariant environments. We show that such environments are universally described by unparticle baths characterized by a single scaling dimension $d_{\mathcal{U}}$. This work provides the proof of the uniqueness theorem, the formalism of the resulting non-Markovian dynamics, and applications to several physical systems.   From the uniqueness theorem, we derive the non-Markovian memory kernels, the exact noise kernel including vacuum and thermal contributions, and a fractional generalization of the Caldeira-Leggett master equation for arbitrary $d_{\mathcal{U}}$. The scaling dimension governs a rich phase structure, including a thermalization transition at $d_{\mathcal{U}}=3/2$, the Ohmic boundary at $d_{\mathcal{U}}=2$, and a decoherence transition at $d_{\mathcal{U}}=5/2$ in the thermal regime, beyond which long-time quantum coherence is protected.   Three realizations are studied. For the quantum Ising model at criticality, coupling to the energy operator in $(1+1)$ dimensions gives $d_{\mathcal{U}}=3/2$, producing $1/f$ noise, while the $(2+1)$D case yields $d_{\mathcal{U}}\approx1.413$ from the conformal bootstrap. In inflationary cosmology, massless scalar and graviton baths in de Sitter spacetime give $d_{\mathcal{U}}=2$, predicting linear decoherence growth consistent with the quantum-to-classical transition. For high-energy astrophysical neutrinos, the decoherence rate $Γ_{\mathrm{decoh}}\propto \mathcal{B}(E,T_{\mathcal{U}})L^{5-2d_{\mathcal{U}}}$ provides an observable signature of the scaling dimension.   We also compare the framework with Caldeira-Leggett and Lindblad approaches, analyze the validity regimes, and discuss experimental implications for trapped-ion simulators, neutrino telescopes, and superconducting qubits.
summary: This paper establishes that all scale-invariant quantum environments are mathematically equivalent to 'unparticle' baths. It provides a generalized non-Markovian master equation that predicts new phase transitions in decoherence and thermalization across condensed matter, cosmology, and particle physics.
main_problem: Developing a complete theoretical framework for open quantum systems coupled to scale-invariant environments and proving that such environments are universally described by unparticle baths.
main_result: The authors prove a uniqueness theorem establishing that scale-invariant, Lorentz-invariant, and unitary environments are uniquely described by unparticle baths, and they identify a rich phase structure including thermalization and decoherence transitions.
method: The work uses the Kallen-Lehmann spectral representation, path integral formalism (influence functionals), and conformal bootstrap to derive non-Markovian master equations and scaling laws.
model_or_system: The framework applies to a system coupled to an unparticle bath characterized by a scaling dimension d_U, with specific realizations in the quantum Ising model, inflationary cosmology (de Sitter space), and high-energy astrophysical neutrinos.
key_observables: Decoherence rate (Gamma_decoh), spectral density J(omega), noise and dissipation kernels, resistivity, and specific heat scaling.
important_parameters: Unparticle scaling dimension (d_U), scaling dimension (Delta), temperature (T), and the spectral exponent (s).
main_assumptions: Local coupling, Lorentz invariance, scale invariance, and unitarity.
figures_summary: The paper includes tables summarizing scaling exponents and dynamical regimes, and figures illustrating the relationship between unparticle dimensions and physical observables.
paper_structure: The paper begins with a proof of the uniqueness theorem, develops the mathematical formalism for non-Markovian dynamics, compares the framework to Caldeira-Leggett and Lindblad models, and concludes with applications to the Ising model, cosmology, and neutrino physics.
why_interesting: It provides a universal, unified language for understanding non-Markovian dynamics across vastly different scales, from superconducting qubits and trapped ions to the early universe and high-energy astrophysics, while predicting new regimes of coherence protection.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
